Your Pre-Prelaunch, Part 1
!
The pre-prelaunch or as we’d like say, call it the pre-pre and as the name implies, this
happens before your pre-launch even starts.
!
This is really under the radar before anyone even knows what’s going on. 	Generally,
people don’t have a hint what you’re really leading into. 	This is the pre-pre, this is
where you really identify objections and gather testimonials.
Your going to want to use the pre-prelaunch to warm your list. 	You might even start
attracting partners and really, it’s about starting to build buzz ahead of the launch. 	This
is where you lay the groundwork for your launch.
!
We usually start off with what I call “the shot across the bow.” 	This is an old naval
term and it would be like, if one navy ship is chasing down another ship or maybe the
coastguard’s ship is chasing down a boat, they would often fire a shot in front of the
other boat, land the shot in the water just to warn them that someone is coming so I
like to call this “the shot across the bow.” 	The essence of the pre-pre is that you’re
letting your market know that something is coming but you’re not actually trying to tell
them.
!
In fact, a big part of message is that the product is not quite ready yet. 	If your product
isn't even ready, you can’t actually be selling it yet, right? 	So you remember that BS
Detector that we talked about in the sideways sales letter? The BS Detector, there’s that
red zone, you want to avoid that red zone and when in you’re in the pre-pre, you’ll see
that it’s very easy to avoid that red zone in the pre-pre. 	Be sure you’re not actually
even selling anything to anyone and when you start off with that BS Detector in the
safe zone, it’s easier to keep it in that safe zone.
!
When there’s no hint of a pitch and you tell people that something is coming and you’re
really excited about it, it’s like you might come out and say, “Hey, we’ve been working
on this thing for years. 	We’re so excited. I can’t wait to bring it to you. 	Can you just
help us out by filling out this survey before we release this?”
!
That is a lot friendlier than something like, “Hey, we’ve been working on this thing for
years. 	I'm really excited about it. 	Please buy it for me, give me your money.” 	So it’s a
lot softer and gentler to just often go out and ask for feedback in the market and that’s
usually the essence of the pre-pre. 	Let’s talk about the characteristics of buzz, because
every market is different with buzz. 	Some markets are more e-mail-centric, some
markets are more forum-centric, some markets are more social media or even live
event-centric.
!
Every market is different and your market will be different from mine. 	In the internet
marketing world, where you teach people about building online businesses, that’s the
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 2
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 2 of 6 --

market that I primarily sell into now. 	In that market, it’s a very connected market and
everyone is on multiple list. 	People go to forums, they all do social media. 	It’s a very
social media-centric market where everyone has got a social media of present and most
of them are publishing via social media. 	They’re all on Twitter, they’re all on Facebook.
It’s a super connected market so I need to take that into account when I'm doing my
pre-prelaunch. Your market may vary.
!
Buzz travels most easily through channels that have trust and authenticity. They’re not
interested in sharing your advertising or if you’re trying to sell them, they’re not going
to go and tell their buddies about your sales. 	They’re not going to tell their buddies
about your marking. It comes down to really creating value and being very, very
interactive 	and 	being 	authentic. 	The 	more 	authentic 	you 	are 	and 	the 	more 	you
communicate in these authentic channels that they have trust, the quicker the buzz is
going to spread. I like to always hit multiple modalities in my pre-pre launch.
!
A lot of people notice something is coming, I love if I can get on stage and speak on
stage somewhere and I can get to do interviews, maybe I drop a hint via e-mail, maybe
I drop a hint via social media. We will leave a trail of bread crumbs for people to follow,
because some people are going to be paying closer attention and most of the people
you want to reach and start having down spread the buzz. 	I’d like to think of this in
terms of concentric circles here. 	It’s my business, me and team right in the middle.
I'm the center of the universe, right? You’re the center of your universe.
!
When I go out there, in terms of influence, the closest people I’ve got are my Owners,
my PLF Owners. 	One of the rules of my business, when we don’t talk about clients, we
don’t about customers, we talk about our Owners and when we talk about our Owners,
this one of the very few rules I have in my company is, you always have to capitalize
the word “Owners,” when we’re writing about our owners.
!
In any case, my PLF Owners are the people that I have the closest connection with.
They have been through my training, they love the training, they’ve gotten good
results.
They generally have great affinity to me, I love them. 	We have this great thing going
with the Owners. 	So usually, my first hint, my first shot across the bow is that
something is coming will often go out to my Owners. 	I’ve got my Owners on the e-mail
list. 	I’ve got my Owners, all in the PLF portal, so I’ll drop a hint that something is
coming in the portal.
!
Now, beyond that, the next level is often for me, it’s my joint venture partners, my JVs.
!
This is only going to be if I'm in a JV launch but I’ll let them know that something is
coming and when I let them know that something is coming, they often start. They
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 6 --

might start Tweeting about it or certain create a blog post about it and they’ll help
create some of the buzz.
!
The next level beyond that is my social media following.
!
In general, I consider an e-mail far more powerful than social media, and I have a
much bigger e-mail list. 	The people that are following me via social media, not all of
them, but a lot of them just don’t actually don’t care, but my most rabid followers of
fans are following me via social media AND will also be on my e-mail list.
!
Social media is inherently very shareable. 	I’ll often drop some hints in social media and
in terms of concentric circles, owners are the closest for me, JVs are the next closest,
social media is after that then comes my list, my prospect list.
I’ll often drop a hint out to them and then, we get beyond that and we get to the
people on my JVs list. 	And then, beyond that is the market in general. 	Everyone in the
market who, maybe if I'm pitching about online marketing, they are not on my JVs list,
they are not on my list, they are not following me, they don’t even know about me but
they’re in the overall market and then, here is the whole rest of the world, probably not
even interested in marketing or business or anything like that. 	When I am going into
pre-prelaunch, I'm mostly going to focus right here on owners, I'm going to focus on
social media, I’ll drop some hints to JVs.
!
None of us are really expecting a ton out of that and then, I’ll just hint something in my
list.
!
I'm not going out to my JVs’ list but what I want, what I'm hoping is that some of these
owners, they’ll start to buzz and they’ll now start to bring some influence out into the
social media area and then some of these social media people, they might start buzzing
and they might start to create some low-lying discussion about what’s going on with my
upcoming launch.
!
You always have to think of who you influence and the people that are closest to you.
!
The people in these inner rings are the people that, not only are most interested in
what you have to share but they’re going to be the most interested in actually going out
and sharing it.
!
Now, one of the things is, people like to support what they help create. 	This is a key
principle.
!
People like to support that what they help create.
!
So if they give you some feedback, if I go out to my people, to my followers and say,
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module4v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 4 of 6 --

!
“Hey, I'm almost done with this product but I want to know, I want to make sure I
cover everything in this product. 	Can you tell me what your number one question is
about growing a list, about learning how to play rhythm guitar, about how to build your
massage tracks?”
!
Whatever 	niche you are in, if you go out and ask people for feedback and what they
need to learn to really help change your lives, then by giving you feedback, you’ve
enlisted them in the cost.
!
They are now part of the creation and people like to support that which they helped
create.
!
If they feel like they’re helping you create a product, they’re going to be more inclined
to tell other people about what you’re doing, to talk about you, to rush to your defense
if anyone is saying bad things about you and then eventually, to get involved in your
whole pre-launch process and then maybe even buy your product.
!
That feedback early on is really, really important. In your pre-prelaunch, a little mystery
goes a long away. 	A lot of times, I’ll structure my pre-prelaunch so I'm dropping bread
crumbs here and there and people try to piece it together and once they piece it
together, then it’s almost like a scavenger hunt and once they start to put the pieces
together, they love to tell others about it.
!
They love having the feeling that they have some insider knowledge... but having
insider knowledge is only great if you can actually go and share it with someone.
!
So people are going to feel great about connecting the breadcrumbs together and now,
they’ve got a secret and then they want to go out and share it.
!
If you can string these breadcrumbs together and create a little mystery, it can go a
long way towards creating, getting the buzz in your launch, in your pre-prelaunch.
!
I like to think about like in Malcolm Glawell’s book, The Tipping Point, he talked about
Mavens and Connectors, and Mavens are people that just love to collect data and
information, they’ve already become expert. 	Connectors are people that they know lots
and lots and lots of other people. They have big followings in social media.
!
A 	lot 	of 	people 	that 	you 	see 	publishing 	very 	actively 	in 	social 	media, 	they 	are
connectors, they really enjoy being connectors. 	Those Mavens and Connectors are
people that I'm trying to get hooked into my pre-pre and get them excited.
!
I am looking for persons of influence within my market or niche. 	These days, almost
everyone is a publisher so who’s got the real influence? 	Who publishes the blogs or
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 5

-- 5 of 6 --

social media? 	How can I get them involved in my pre-pre? 	How can I turn them into
champions and throw at least some breadcrumbs for them, or maybe it’s more overt
with them. 	People love to know a secret. 	People love scarce information and if they
have scarce information, they love to tell other people about it and that all helps to
build the buzz.
Now, I know I'm at the thirty thousand-foot level on this but we’re about to drill down
really deep. 	I have some examples for you in the next few videos. 	You’ll see how I’ve
done this, how I used the pre-prelaunch in my launches and how effective they’ve
been. 	You’ll see how you’ve done it a variety of ways and gotten different effects from
it.
